(P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市横田町字治道23', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Honden1.jpg|270px]]
Honden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}Yokota-chō, Aza Harumichi 23 |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former designated {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}・copper-plate roof |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}}: {{ill|lt=Gion festival|Q979873|WD=Q979873|1=Gion Matsuri}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}
{{ill|lt=September 13|Q2848|WD=Q2848|1=September 13}} Annual festival{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 10.89 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 28.04 |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Wanishita Shrine'''（わにしたじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yokota-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; under the former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and it was also a {{ill|lt=beihei-sha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine receiving offerings|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=54}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined at the center{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === History === It is located on the south bank about 1.5 {{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|1=Kilometre}} from the confluence of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takase River (Nara)|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wani River|1=UNKNOWN}}, facing the old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tatsuta Road|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|Q59282589|WD=Q59282589|lt=Wanishita Shrine|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, about 2.5 km east, this shrine is also called Shimoharumichi-no-miya (or Shimojidō-gu) and Shimoharumichi Tennō{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. It is regarded as one of the two seats of '''Wajirimo Shrine''' listed in {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491|1=Soekami District, Nara}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. The area around the shrine is believed to have been the base of the Sakurai clan, a branch of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873|1=Wani clan}}, and artifacts dating to the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} have been excavated from Yokota Shimoike north of the shrine. It is thought this shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deity of the Sakurai clan, and that its source deity may have been the Wani clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame no Tarashihiko Kunioshiwake no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. It is said the ujiko extended as far south as {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}'s {{ill|lt=Hōsono Village|Q30937308|WD=Q30937308|1=Hōsono}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === Precincts === [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Keidai.jpg|thumb|200px|Precincts]] === Honden === A two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-plate roofing{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}. Records state that the former shrine building of Mizutani Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, was transferred here in Kan'ei 10 ({{ill|lt=1633|Q6870|WD=Q6870|1=1633}}){{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}. === Sub-shrines === ; East of main sanctuary * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Konpira-gū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Amaterasu Kōtai Jingū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kasuga Daimyōjin{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} ; West of main sanctuaryThis list follows the Nara Prefectural History description, but as of August 2018 differs from the actual sub-shrines on site. Specifically, Suwa and Ebisu shrines are not present, and instead Itsukushima, Komori, and Kōra shrines are enshrined. * Hachiman Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Hakusan Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Suwa Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Ebisu Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kifune Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Yamato/Kii Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|寺院神社大事典}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928859692}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail38.html Wanishita Shrine] - Yamatokōriyama Tourism Association {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:春日移し|摂]]

[[commons:Category:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)]][[ja:和爾下神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{Expand Japanese|和爾下神社 (大和郡山市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593) | native_name = 和爾下神社 | image = Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Honden1.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593) | coordinates = {{coord|34.619633|135.80778}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Wanishita Shrine'''|和爾下神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. {{ill|Q135038818|ltq=Q135038818|WD=Q135038818|1=Wanishimono Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市横田町字治道23', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Honden1.jpg|270px]]
Honden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}Yokota-chō, Aza Harumichi 23 |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former designated {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}・copper-plate roof |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}}: {{ill|lt=Gion festival|Q979873|WD=Q979873|1=Gion Matsuri}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}
{{ill|lt=September 13|Q2848|WD=Q2848|1=September 13}} Annual festival{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 10.89 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 28.04 |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Wanishita Shrine'''（わにしたじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yokota-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; under the former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and it was also a {{ill|lt=beihei-sha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine receiving offerings|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=54}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined at the center{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === History === It is located on the south bank about 1.5 {{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|1=Kilometre}} from the confluence of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takase River (Nara)|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wani River|1=UNKNOWN}}, facing the old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tatsuta Road|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|Q59282589|WD=Q59282589|lt=Wanishita Shrine|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, about 2.5 km east, this shrine is also called Shimoharumichi-no-miya (or Shimojidō-gu) and Shimoharumichi Tennō{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. It is regarded as one of the two seats of '''Wajirimo Shrine''' listed in {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491|1=Soekami District, Nara}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. The area around the shrine is believed to have been the base of the Sakurai clan, a branch of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873|1=Wani clan}}, and artifacts dating to the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} have been excavated from Yokota Shimoike north of the shrine. It is thought this shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deity of the Sakurai clan, and that its source deity may have been the Wani clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame no Tarashihiko Kunioshiwake no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. It is said the ujiko extended as far south as {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}'s {{ill|lt=Hōsono Village|Q30937308|WD=Q30937308|1=Hōsono}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === Precincts === [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Keidai.jpg|thumb|200px|Precincts]] === Honden === A two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-plate roofing{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}. Records state that the former shrine building of Mizutani Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, was transferred here in Kan'ei 10 ({{ill|lt=1633|Q6870|WD=Q6870|1=1633}}){{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}. === Sub-shrines === ; East of main sanctuary * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Konpira-gū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Amaterasu Kōtai Jingū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kasuga Daimyōjin{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} ; West of main sanctuaryThis list follows the Nara Prefectural History description, but as of August 2018 differs from the actual sub-shrines on site. Specifically, Suwa and Ebisu shrines are not present, and instead Itsukushima, Komori, and Kōra shrines are enshrined. * Hachiman Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Hakusan Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Suwa Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Ebisu Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kifune Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Yamato/Kii Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|寺院神社大事典}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928859692}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail38.html Wanishita Shrine] - Yamatokōriyama Tourism Association {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:History of Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:春日移し|摂]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市横田町字治道23', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Honden1.jpg|270px]]
Honden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}Yokota-chō, Aza Harumichi 23 |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former designated {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}・copper-plate roof |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}}: {{ill|lt=Gion festival|Q979873|WD=Q979873|1=Gion Matsuri}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}
{{ill|lt=September 13|Q2848|WD=Q2848|1=September 13}} Annual festival{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 10.89 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 28.04 |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Wanishita Shrine'''（わにしたじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yokota-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; under the former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and it was also a {{ill|lt=beihei-sha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine receiving offerings|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=54}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined at the center{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === History === It is located on the south bank about 1.5 {{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|1=Kilometre}} from the confluence of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takase River (Nara)|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wani River|1=UNKNOWN}}, facing the old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tatsuta Road|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|Q59282589|WD=Q59282589|lt=Wanishita Shrine|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, about 2.5 km east, this shrine is also called Shimoharumichi-no-miya (or Shimojidō-gu) and Shimoharumichi Tennō{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. It is regarded as one of the two seats of '''Wajirimo Shrine''' listed in {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491|1=Soekami District, Nara}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. The area around the shrine is believed to have been the base of the Sakurai clan, a branch of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873|1=Wani clan}}, and artifacts dating to the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} have been excavated from Yokota Shimoike north of the shrine. It is thought this shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deity of the Sakurai clan, and that its source deity may have been the Wani clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame no Tarashihiko Kunioshiwake no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. It is said the ujiko extended as far south as {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}'s {{ill|lt=Hōsono Village|Q30937308|WD=Q30937308|1=Hōsono}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === Precincts === [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Keidai.jpg|thumb|200px|Precincts]] === Honden === A two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-plate roofing{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}. Records state that the former shrine building of Mizutani Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, was transferred here in Kan'ei 10 ({{ill|lt=1633|Q6870|WD=Q6870|1=1633}}){{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}. === Sub-shrines === ; East of main sanctuary * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Konpira-gū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Amaterasu Kōtai Jingū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kasuga Daimyōjin{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} ; West of main sanctuaryThis list follows the Nara Prefectural History description, but as of August 2018 differs from the actual sub-shrines on site. Specifically, Suwa and Ebisu shrines are not present, and instead Itsukushima, Komori, and Kōra shrines are enshrined. * Hachiman Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Hakusan Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Suwa Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Ebisu Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kifune Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Yamato/Kii Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|寺院神社大事典}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928859692}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail38.html Wanishita Shrine] - Yamatokōriyama Tourism Association {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:History of Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:春日移し|摂]]

{{ill|Q135038818|ltq=Q135038818|WD=Q135038818|1=Wanishimono Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市横田町字治道23', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Honden1.jpg|270px]]
Honden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}Yokota-chō, Aza Harumichi 23 |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former designated {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}・copper-plate roof |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}}: {{ill|lt=Gion festival|Q979873|WD=Q979873|1=Gion Matsuri}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}
{{ill|lt=September 13|Q2848|WD=Q2848|1=September 13}} Annual festival{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 10.89 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 28.04 |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Wanishita Shrine'''（わにしたじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yokota-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; under the former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and it was also a {{ill|lt=beihei-sha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine receiving offerings|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=54}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined at the center{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === History === It is located on the south bank about 1.5 {{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|1=Kilometre}} from the confluence of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takase River (Nara)|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wani River|1=UNKNOWN}}, facing the old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tatsuta Road|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|Q59282589|WD=Q59282589|lt=Wanishita Shrine|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, about 2.5 km east, this shrine is also called Shimoharumichi-no-miya (or Shimojidō-gu) and Shimoharumichi Tennō{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. It is regarded as one of the two seats of '''Wajirimo Shrine''' listed in {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491|1=Soekami District, Nara}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. The area around the shrine is believed to have been the base of the Sakurai clan, a branch of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873|1=Wani clan}}, and artifacts dating to the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} have been excavated from Yokota Shimoike north of the shrine. It is thought this shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deity of the Sakurai clan, and that its source deity may have been the Wani clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame no Tarashihiko Kunioshiwake no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. It is said the ujiko extended as far south as {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}'s {{ill|lt=Hōsono Village|Q30937308|WD=Q30937308|1=Hōsono}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === Precincts === [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Keidai.jpg|thumb|200px|Precincts]] === Honden === A two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-plate roofing{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}. Records state that the former shrine building of Mizutani Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, was transferred here in Kan'ei 10 ({{ill|lt=1633|Q6870|WD=Q6870|1=1633}}){{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}. === Sub-shrines === ; East of main sanctuary * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Konpira-gū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Amaterasu Kōtai Jingū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kasuga Daimyōjin{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} ; West of main sanctuaryThis list follows the Nara Prefectural History description, but as of August 2018 differs from the actual sub-shrines on site. Specifically, Suwa and Ebisu shrines are not present, and instead Itsukushima, Komori, and Kōra shrines are enshrined. * Hachiman Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Hakusan Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Suwa Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Ebisu Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kifune Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Yamato/Kii Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|寺院神社大事典}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928859692}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail38.html Wanishita Shrine] - Yamatokōriyama Tourism Association {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:History of Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:春日移し|摂]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}Yokota-chō, Aza Harumichi 23 |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former designated {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}・copper-plate roof |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}}: {{ill|lt=Gion festival|Q979873|WD=Q979873|1=Gion Matsuri}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}
{{ill|lt=September 13|Q2848|WD=Q2848|1=September 13}} Annual festival{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 10.89 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 28.04 |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Wanishita Shrine'''（わにしたじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yokota-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; under the former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and it was also a {{ill|lt=beihei-sha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine receiving offerings|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=54}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined at the center{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === History === It is located on the south bank about 1.5 {{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|1=Kilometre}} from the confluence of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takase River (Nara)|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wani River|1=UNKNOWN}}, facing the old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tatsuta Road|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|Q59282589|WD=Q59282589|lt=Wanishita Shrine|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, about 2.5 km east, this shrine is also called Shimoharumichi-no-miya (or Shimojidō-gu) and Shimoharumichi Tennō{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. It is regarded as one of the two seats of '''Wajirimo Shrine''' listed in {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491|1=Soekami District, Nara}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. The area around the shrine is believed to have been the base of the Sakurai clan, a branch of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873|1=Wani clan}}, and artifacts dating to the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} have been excavated from Yokota Shimoike north of the shrine. It is thought this shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deity of the Sakurai clan, and that its source deity may have been the Wani clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame no Tarashihiko Kunioshiwake no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. It is said the ujiko extended as far south as {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}'s {{ill|lt=Hōsono Village|Q30937308|WD=Q30937308|1=Hōsono}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === Precincts === [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Keidai.jpg|thumb|200px|Precincts]] === Honden === A two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-plate roofing{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}. Records state that the former shrine building of Mizutani Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, was transferred here in Kan'ei 10 ({{ill|lt=1633|Q6870|WD=Q6870|1=1633}}){{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}. === Sub-shrines === ; East of main sanctuary * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Konpira-gū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Amaterasu Kōtai Jingū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kasuga Daimyōjin{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} ; West of main sanctuaryThis list follows the Nara Prefectural History description, but as of August 2018 differs from the actual sub-shrines on site. Specifically, Suwa and Ebisu shrines are not present, and instead Itsukushima, Komori, and Kōra shrines are enshrined. * Hachiman Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Hakusan Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Suwa Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Ebisu Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kifune Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Yamato/Kii Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|寺院神社大事典}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928859692}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail38.html Wanishita Shrine] - Yamatokōriyama Tourism Association {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:History of Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]]

{{ill|Q135038818|ltq=Q135038818|WD=Q135038818|1=Wanishimono Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市横田町字治道23', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282593}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Honden1.jpg|270px]]
Honden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}Yokota-chō, Aza Harumichi 23 |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former designated {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}・copper-plate roof |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}}: {{ill|lt=Gion festival|Q979873|WD=Q979873|1=Gion Matsuri}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}
{{ill|lt=September 13|Q2848|WD=Q2848|1=September 13}} Annual festival{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 10.89 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 28.04 |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Wanishita Shrine'''（わにしたじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yokota-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; under the former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and it was also a {{ill|lt=beihei-sha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine receiving offerings|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=54}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined at the center{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === History === It is located on the south bank about 1.5 {{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|1=Kilometre}} from the confluence of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takase River (Nara)|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wani River|1=UNKNOWN}}, facing the old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tatsuta Road|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|Q59282589|WD=Q59282589|lt=Wanishita Shrine|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, about 2.5 km east, this shrine is also called Shimoharumichi-no-miya (or Shimojidō-gu) and Shimoharumichi Tennō{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. It is regarded as one of the two seats of '''Wajirimo Shrine''' listed in {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491|1=Soekami District, Nara}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. The area around the shrine is believed to have been the base of the Sakurai clan, a branch of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873|1=Wani clan}}, and artifacts dating to the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} have been excavated from Yokota Shimoike north of the shrine. It is thought this shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deity of the Sakurai clan, and that its source deity may have been the Wani clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame no Tarashihiko Kunioshiwake no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. It is said the ujiko extended as far south as {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}'s {{ill|lt=Hōsono Village|Q30937308|WD=Q30937308|1=Hōsono}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === Precincts === [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Keidai.jpg|thumb|200px|Precincts]] === Honden === A two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-plate roofing{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}. Records state that the former shrine building of Mizutani Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, was transferred here in Kan'ei 10 ({{ill|lt=1633|Q6870|WD=Q6870|1=1633}}){{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}. === Sub-shrines === ; East of main sanctuary * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Konpira-gū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Amaterasu Kōtai Jingū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kasuga Daimyōjin{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} ; West of main sanctuaryThis list follows the Nara Prefectural History description, but as of August 2018 differs from the actual sub-shrines on site. Specifically, Suwa and Ebisu shrines are not present, and instead Itsukushima, Komori, and Kōra shrines are enshrined. * Hachiman Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Hakusan Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Suwa Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Ebisu Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kifune Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Yamato/Kii Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|寺院神社大事典}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928859692}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail38.html Wanishita Shrine] - Yamatokōriyama Tourism Association {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:History of Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]]

Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Honden1.jpg|270px]]
Honden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}Yokota-chō, Aza Harumichi 23 |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former designated {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}・copper-plate roof |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}}: {{ill|lt=Gion festival|Q979873|WD=Q979873|1=Gion Matsuri}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}
{{ill|lt=September 13|Q2848|WD=Q2848|1=September 13}} Annual festival{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 10.89 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 28.04 |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Wanishita Shrine'''（わにしたじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yokota-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; under the former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and it was also a {{ill|lt=beihei-sha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine receiving offerings|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=54}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined at the center{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === History === It is located on the south bank about 1.5 {{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|1=Kilometre}} from the confluence of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takase River (Nara)|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wani River|1=UNKNOWN}}, facing the old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tatsuta Road|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|Q59282589|WD=Q59282589|lt=Wanishita Shrine|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, about 2.5 km east, this shrine is also called Shimoharumichi-no-miya (or Shimojidō-gu) and Shimoharumichi Tennō{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. It is regarded as one of the two seats of '''Wajirimo Shrine''' listed in {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491|1=Soekami District, Nara}}{{sfn|寺院神社大事典|p=832}}. The area around the shrine is believed to have been the base of the Sakurai clan, a branch of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873|1=Wani clan}}, and artifacts dating to the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} have been excavated from Yokota Shimoike north of the shrine. It is thought this shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deity of the Sakurai clan, and that its source deity may have been the Wani clan ancestor {{ill|lt=Ame no Tarashihiko Kunioshiwake no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. It is said the ujiko extended as far south as {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}'s {{ill|lt=Hōsono Village|Q30937308|WD=Q30937308|1=Hōsono}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=329}}. === Precincts === [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Yokota) Keidai.jpg|thumb|200px|Precincts]] === Honden === A two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-plate roofing{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}}. Records state that the former shrine building of Mizutani Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, was transferred here in Kan'ei 10 ({{ill|lt=1633|Q6870|WD=Q6870|1=1633}}){{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}. === Sub-shrines === ; East of main sanctuary * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Konpira-gū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Amaterasu Kōtai Jingū{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kasuga Daimyōjin{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} ; West of main sanctuaryThis list follows the Nara Prefectural History description, but as of August 2018 differs from the actual sub-shrines on site. Specifically, Suwa and Ebisu shrines are not present, and instead Itsukushima, Komori, and Kōra shrines are enshrined. * Hachiman Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Hakusan Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Suwa Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Ebisu Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} * Kifune Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史|p=304}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Yamato/Kii Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|寺院神社大事典}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928859692}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Yokota)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail38.html Wanishita Shrine] - Yamatokōriyama Tourism Association {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanishita Shrine (Q59282593)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:History of Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]]
